summary: Fixed the Explore voice-count TypeScript error, added a reusable voice mapping modal, connected player mapping/download/error states, extended BookMetadata schema for backend progress/persona fields, and improved library progress calculation.

tests.notes: Expo web export completed; expo-av deprecation warning only.
go binary not installed in Hermes environment.

git.commit_message_suggestion: feat(ui): add voice mapping flow and stabilize web client

blockers: Go backend tests blocked because go is not installed.
risks: VoiceMappingModal submits initial=true for full mappings; backend defaults to initial when absent and supports this path for waiting voice_mapping state.
Expo AV is deprecated in SDK warning but existing app already uses expo-av; migration to expo-audio is a future task.
follow_up_suggestions: Run Go tests in an environment with Go installed.
Exercise the voice_mapping flow against a live backend with a book paused for mapping.
Consider adding component/unit tests if a React Native testing setup is introduced.

questions: Do the UI changes satisfy compile and core voice-mapping acceptance?
Are there API contract risks in the new modal and schema extensions?
